2605.12733.title: From Generalist to Specialist Representation
2605.12733.paper_summary: Given a generalist model, learning a task-relevant specialist representation is fundamental for downstream applications. Identifiability, the asymptotic guarantee of recovering the ground-truth representation, is critical because it sets the ultimate limit of any model, even with infinite data and computation. We study this problem in a completely nonparametric setting, without relying on interventions, parametric forms, or structural constraints. We first prove that the structure between time steps and tasks is identifiable in a fully unsupervised manner, even when sequences lack strict temporal dependence and may exhibit disconnections, and task assignments can follow arbitrarily complex and interleaving structures. We then prove that, within each time step, the task-relevant latent representation can be disentangled from the irrelevant part under a simple sparsity regularization, without any additional information or parametric constraints. Together, these results establish a hierarchical foundation: task structure is identifiable across time steps, and task-relevant latent representations are identifiable within each step. To our knowledge, each result provides a first general nonparametric identifiability guarantee, and together they mark a step toward provably moving from generalist to specialist models.
2605.12733.paper_summary_zh: 給定一個通用模型，學習與任務相關的專家表示對於下游應用是基本的。可識別性，即恢復真實表示的漸近保證，是至關重要的，因為它設置了任何模型的最終限制，即使擁有無限的數據和計算。我們在一個完全非參數的環境中研究這個問題，無需依賴干預、參數形式或結構約束。我們首先證明了時間步驟與任務之間的結構在完全無監督的方式下是可識別的，即使序列缺乏嚴格的時間依賴性並可能出現斷裂，且任務分配可以遵循任意複雜和交錯的結構。我們接著證明，在每個時間步驟內，與任務相關的潛在表示可以在簡單的稀疏正則化下與無關部分解開，而無需任何額外的信息或參數約束。這些結果共同建立了一個層次基礎：任務結構在時間步驟之間是可識別的，與任務相關的潛在表示在每個步驟內是可識別的。據我們所知，每個結果提供了第一個一般的非參數可識別性保證，並且它們共同標誌著從通用模型到專家模型的可證明進步。
2605.12733.author: Yujia Zheng et.al.
2605.12733.authors: Yujia Zheng, Fan Feng, Yuke Li, Shaoan Xie, Kevin Murphy, Kun Zhang
